nativeRange.habitat: Found on coral reefs, where it tends to perch motionlessly on the outer branches of stony corals such as Stylophora, Pocillopora, and Acropora, at depths of 1–35 m.

diet.notes: Has been documented consuming small fishes, shrimp, crabs, and other crustaceans. Generally accepts frozen meaty foods such as mysis shrimp, krill, and marine meats in captivity.

summary: The Freckled Hawkfish is a boldly freckled, ambush-perching predator from the Indo-Pacific, generally considered intermediate in care and best introduced last into a semi-aggressive to aggressive community.
careNotes:
The Freckled Hawkfish (Paracirrhites forsteri) is known to perch motionlessly on coral branches and rockwork, waiting to ambush prey — a behavior that tends to make it highly territorial toward new tankmates, including fish considerably larger than itself. It is generally advised to introduce it as the last fish in a semi-aggressive to aggressive community. Multiple specimens have been known to cohabit peacefully for extended periods before suddenly becoming combative, so keeping more than one is generally not recommended.

Juveniles have been reported to display a burgundy body with a yellow tail; as adults, the coloration tends to shift to pink, brown, or olive, with consistent black or red freckled markings on the head. The species is territorial and haremic in the wild, with a dominant male presiding over a group of females on the reef.

Regarding reef safety: it is generally considered reef-safe with caution. Corals are typically left undisturbed, but ornamental shrimp (cleaner shrimp, peppermint shrimp) and small crabs are at significant risk of predation. Small fish tankmates are also vulnerable.

The care level is rated intermediate primarily because of the need to manage its aggression and its incompatibility with many common reef invertebrates, rather than any particular sensitivity to water quality.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species-specific product page. Provided size (9 in), tank size (70 gal), temperature (72–78°F), pH (8.1–8.4), SG (1.020–1.025), dKH (8–12), care level (Easy), temperament (Aggressive), reef compatibility (With Caution).

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Secondary cross-check. Confirmed adult size 22 cm, minimum tank 300 L, carnivore diet. Noted as 'very aggressive' and haremic. Original secondary URL (Reef2Reef thread) returned HTTP 403; ReefApp was used instead.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed maximum length 22.0 cm TL, common length 18.0 cm. Wild temperature 24–27°C. Taxonomy: Family Cirrhitidae, Order Centrarchiformes. Confirmed territorial and haremic behavior. Indo-Pacific native range from Red Sea/East Africa to Hawaii and Japan.

additional.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Confirmed adult size ~8.5 in (21.6 cm), 70 gal minimum, carnivore, aggressive, reef safe with caution. Care level listed as Beginner — differs from intermediate rating used here, which reflects aggression management.
